title: autobench.rsi.checkpoint_revert v1
description: Emitted (nervous-bus-sf0y) when an RSI iteration scored more than ``variance_floor`` below the best-so-far iter and the loop reverted its working harness to the best-iter checkpoint before starting the next iteration. Prevents compounding wrong-direction improver edits — after a >2σ regression the loop snaps back to the known-good harness AND the improver's next call gets revert context so it can reconsider its hypothesis instead of doubling down on the failed edit. Source: /autobench.

iter_regressed.description: RSI iteration whose score regressed below (best_score - variance_floor)

reverted_to_iter.description: Iteration whose harness is now the active base for the next iter

variance_floor_used.description: The 2σ noise floor (from AUTOBENCH_VARIANCE_FLOOR or default 0.027) below which we treat the regression as signal not noise

best_score.description: Score of the iteration we're reverting to
